description:
Add a --recursive/-r flag to `bw close` that closes the issue and its entire subtree (all descendants, leaf-up).

New store method CloseSubtree(id, reason) -> SubtreeCloseResult{Closed, Skipped, Unblocked}:
- Walk descendants via buildSubtreeSet; close leaf-up (children before parent).
- Skip already-closed members silently; tolerate already-closed root.
- Close in_progress/deferred members too.
- Reason: root gets --reason verbatim; descendants get auto-note 'closed with parent <root-id>'.
- Aggregate NewlyUnblocked across closed members, dedupe, exclude in-subtree issues.

CLI (cmd/bw/close.go): add --recursive bool flag + CloseArgs.Recursive; single commit via commitWithRetry; intent emits one 'close <id>' line per closed issue + 'unblocked' lines. Non-recursive path unchanged (back-compat CloseResult JSON).

Wiring: add '-r':'--recursive' to helpers.go aliases; add flag+example to command.go close spec.

Tests: close_test.go (recursive, already-closed skipped, -r alias, JSON shape) + issue-level CloseSubtree test (nested grandchildren, external unblock surfaced, internal suppressed).

No confirmation prompt (reversible via reopen). --dry-run deferred.
title: Add recursive close (--recursive/-r) to cascade-close an issue's subtree
